description_long: This object consists of several irregularly shaped stone blocks arranged on a roughly square base slab. The stones vary in size and orientation, with two prominent upright slabs—one tall and narrow, the other broad and flat—rising vertically from the base. Smaller angular stones are scattered around the base, some leaning against the larger slabs, creating a rugged, layered appearance. The surfaces of the stones are rough and matte, with visible chipping and natural textural variations typical of quarried stone. The coloration is a consistent dark gray, suggesting basalt or slate, and there are no visible engravings or artificial markings. The overall form suggests a deliberate but unrefined arrangement, possibly serving as a barrier or boundary marker in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A cluster of rough, dark gray stone blocks arranged on a square base, featuring upright slabs and scattered angular stones with a rugged, natural texture.
description_view_0: Front view shows the tallest upright slab at the back, a broad flat stone to the right, and several smaller blocks clustered at the base.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the broad flat stone dominating the right side, with the tall slab partially obscured and more of the base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizes the back of the broad flat stone and the tall slab, with fewer small blocks visible and a clearer view of the layered base.
description_view_3: Side view reveals the tall, narrow slab most prominently, with the broad stone and smaller blocks receding into the background.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or landscaped park boundary
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on a prepared base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some chipped corners and rough edges, but no significant moss or discoloration; surfaces appear naturally weathered.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, possibly serving as a standalone barrier or marker
